rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #4, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/12/23. (No earlier availability.) (1) Original bank statements verifying the ending balances must be filed. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF ROWAN STRUMMER TELLES
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/19/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Non-cash assets on hand at the end of the accounting period (Schedule E, which illogically follows page 2 of 2 of Schedule A which follows page 2 of 2 of Non-Cash Assets on Hand at Beginning of Account Period which follows Schedule G) differ from the assets on hand at the beginning of the accounting period, but the purchases (changes in the form of assets) are not reflected on Schedule F. (2) The Morgan Stanley account shows 160 shares of Alphabet, not 8. (3) Schedule B1 does not show the source of the short-term gains or for which year the IRS tax refund was. (4) Schedule C still does not show full descriptions. For what year(s) were tax payments made? (5) The still unidentified schedule "Other Credits" does not show the source of the short-term losses. For what years were tax payments reimbursed to Miriam Telles on 10/15/22 and 12/21/22? (6) What was the date of the order authorizing $467.65 fees to her attorney? (7) The order dated 4/5/22 authorizing fees to Ernest Der in this matter was for $685.00 + $60 reimbursement for costs. That order also approved $8,611.50 for his services as the guardian ad litem in the minor's father's estate + $915.00 reimbursement for costs. How did the guardian arrive at $8,199.00 on Schedule G? (And she reportedly made the payment on 12/21/21?) (8) Exhibit D to this amended petition is not very elucidating. What is it meant to support? (9) There are no local rules 2.10 M.4., 14.70, or 14.100 D. (10) A party's representative capacity is not "Petitioner" - this fiduciary is a guardian of the estate. A SECOND AMENDED ACCOUNTING must be filed. Deadline is 12/8/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Pages in the amended accounting are STILL not in order, making the accounting difficult to review and more susceptible to closer scrutiny. All pages showing assets on hand at the beginning of the accounting period should be together at the beginning of the pages of schedules, all pages of receipts should be together, and all pages of assets on hand at the end of the accounting period should be together. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JULIA C. TANDY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Petitioner failed to disclose that Decedent was predeceased by a sibling, who was survived by issue. 2) Petitioner lived in the estate real property after Decedent's death without payment of rent. 3) Petitioner entered into a rent back agreement with the buyer of the real property, in the amount of $4,200. The funds were paid from the estate, rather than from Petitioner, personally, and appears should be treated as an unauthorized preliminary distribution. 4) Petitioner requests that $907.50 be deducted from his statutory fees for costs to prepare fiduciary accounting. However, a total of $1,546.25 was or will be paid to the account preparer. 5) The liabilities schedule, which is used to calculate the proposed distribution, appears to be incorrect. $18,169.94 + $19,077.44 + $907.50 = $38,154.88, not $39,062.38. 6) Petitioner, as administrator of Carl Tandy's estate, is only bonded for $20,000, which will be insufficient once distribution is made to Carl's estate. 7) Notice of proposed action on sale of real property was never sent to the heirs of Carl Tandy's estate. Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JILL FRANCES GRIFFIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/13/23. (No earlier availability.) 1) As previously noted, the closest living heirs is not the definitive list of heirs. If a first cousin predeceased and was survived by issue, that issue must be identified and must receive notice. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Were Hoyle Glen McManus, J. Edward "Chuck" McManus, Bonnie Eifrd Gray, Donald Ray Shoe, Patricia A. Shoe, Cynthia Fink Jones, Rose Marie Fink Barnett, and Wiliam Ray Fink not survived by issue? 2) Given the incomplete list of paternal heirs, are the maternal second cousins ALL of the maternal heirs, including any more remote issue of predeceased second cousins not identified on Exhibit C to the "Declaration" filed on 10/25/23? 3) Notice was not given to the maternal second cousins or second cousins once removed. Deadline is 12/4/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The number of husbands the decedent's paternal grandmother had is irrelevant; her issue and those of her issue who predeceased this decedent are relevant.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE KWAN K.LEE AND LAN H. LEE 2009 REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Modification Of Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/27/2023, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 08/22/2023) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received. The proposed order must set forth verbatim each trust provision as modified and affected by this petition. 2) Dept number must be stated at item 2 of the DE-120 form. 3) All issue of the remainder beneficiaries must also consent to the proposed modification under Probate Code section 15403 as contingent beneficiaries, even if they are not entitled to notice. See Law Revision Commission Commentary to section 15804. See also section 24(c). It should be noted that the modification was not sought under section 15409 (which does not require consent of all beneficiaries) as an alternate ground. 4) The drafting attorney's "declaration" must include language required by CCP section 2015.5.

rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Nothing has been filed to address the following issues since the last hearing on 10/10/2023: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) No DE-147 form is on file. 3) No DE-147S form is on file. This form must be filed confidentially and conventionally with a pleading cover sheet attached (cannot be e-filed or attached to any e-filed document). 4) No proof of publication is on file. 5) The petitioner's name stated at items 2 and 3 of the DE-121 form must exactly match the name under which this petition was filed (no middle initial). 6) The petition caption: The first box must be checked, as the petitioner is named as executor in the will. 7) Item 2b(1) must be checked. 8) The proof of service attached to the DE-121 form does not show direct notice given to Barbara Meyer. CRC 7.51(a)(1) & (2). No waiver of direct notice signed by her or her attorney is on file. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GEORGE DAVID LIVING TRUST, DATED 7/7/22
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/19/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The only correct form of notice for a petition filed pursuant to Probate Code Section 850 is the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-115, on the reverse side of which is the proof of service that should be completed. (2) Prob. C. Sect. 850 succeeded Heggstad and sets higher standards for proving that assets not transferred to a trust during a trustor's life were, in fact, intended to be held in the trust. In other words, a beneficiary of the trust receives mailed notice with a copy of the petition, but any heir-at-law, who would stand to inherit, if the trust did not exist or were invalid, is a party whose right, title or interest would be affected by this petition, and, therefore, must receive (or waive) personal service of notice. [Prob. C. Sect. 17203(b) and CCP Sect. 413.10 et seq.] (3) Was the grantor the surviving joint tenant of the accounts held in joint tenancy? Please provide courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 12/8/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
